title: Judge denies Lindsay Clancy’s request for two-part murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judge William Sullivan denied Lindsay Clancy's request for a two-part trial for her July murder case.
Clancy, accused of killing her three children in 2023, will face a single trial on July 20.
Her defense argues insanity due to postpartum mental illness and auditory hallucinations before the 2023 incident.

title: What we know about the armed robbery of a massive Beverly mansion
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: An intruder broke into the 'Rock Edge' mansion in Beverly, assaulted a caretaker, and stole valuables and a Porsche.
The caretaker was treated for nonlife-threatening injuries after being tied up and beaten by the masked assailant.
The mansion, built in 1904, previously sold for $18.275 million, making it Beverly's most expensive home sale.

title: Walmart closes Worcester fulfillment center, laying off 90 employees
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Walmart closed its Sam's Club Fulfillment Center in Worcester on Feb. 25, laying off 90 employees.
Affected employees can apply for other company positions during a 90-day paid transition period with a $7,500 transfer incentive.
Closure aims to streamline fulfillment; Walmart opening new Next Generation facility in California this year.

title: Long-planned Nubian Square jazz club finally secures funding
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Jazz Urbane Cafe in Nubian Square secures $2.5 million funding, enabling launch nearly a decade after inception.
The cafe, led by Berklee professor Bill Banfield, will feature live music, a restaurant, and event space in Roxbury.
Funding includes loans from MassDevelopment and M&T Bank, with support from The Boston Foundation and city credits.

title: JetBlue has raised checked bag fees. See how much you’ll pay.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue increased checked bag fees by $4 for off-peak and $9 for peak travel periods due to rising costs.
New checked bag fees are $39 minimum off-peak and $49 minimum during peak, with higher rates if paid within 24 hours of departure.
JetBlue Plus, Business, Premier Cardmembers, and TrueBlue Mosaic members receive certain free checked bag benefits.

title: Mass. man pleads guilty to plotting to assassinate Trump Cabinet member
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Ryan Michael English of South Deerfield admitted to plotting to assassinate then-Treasury Secretary nominee Scott Bessent.
English pleaded guilty to carrying incendiary devices and a knife on Capitol grounds during the Senate vote on January 27, 2025.
English's plea agreement includes charges with maximum sentences of up to 15 years in prison and $260,000 in fines.

title: Salisbury woman found guilty of murdering boyfriend with antifreeze
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church, 67, was found guilty of first-degree murder for poisoning her boyfriend Leroy Fowler in Salisbury.
Church killed Fowler by adding ethylene glycol, found in antifreeze, to his Powerade, according to prosecutors.
Church's sentencing is scheduled for Thursday; she is expected to receive a life sentence for the 2022 murder.

title: New England nominees for James Beard Awards 2026: See chefs, restaurants who made the cut
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: No chefs or restaurants from Massachusetts were nominated for the 2026 James Beard Awards.
All other New England states have nominees, including The Shipwright's Daughter in Mystic, Connecticut.
Winners for each category will be announced on June 15 in Chicago during the annual gala.

title: Dorchester woman wins $2 million on scratch ticket bought at local liquor store
author: John Waller
bullets: Tuyet Le of Dorchester won $2 million on a scratch ticket purchased at Savin Hill Wine & Spirits.
Le elected the cash option, receiving a one-time payment of $1.3 million before taxes.
The liquor store at 1051 Dorchester Ave. earned a $20,000 bonus for selling the winning ticket.

title: She was told she might never walk again. Now she’s running Boston.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Caylie McMahon, once unable to walk after spinal surgery, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Caylie and friend Lindsey Stagg are raising funds for Spaulding Rehabilitation's Race for Rehab team.
The duo's connection to Spaulding Rehabilitation began with their mentor's recovery from a spinal stroke in 2015.
